pattern: An unbounded-knapsack count where iterating coins in the outer loop and amounts inside ensures each combination is counted once regardless of order.
hints: Build up the number of ways to form every amount from zero up to the target.
Looping over coins first and amounts second prevents counting the same multiset of coins in different orders.
Let ways[0] = 1; for each coin, for amount from coin to target, add ways[amount - coin] into ways[amount]; the answer is ways[amount].

edge_inputs.description: amount unreachable with the only coin
amount zero has the empty combination
order should not create extra combinations
all ones gives a single combination
coin larger than amount contributes nothing

stress.description: random distinct coins and a moderate amount within 32-bit safe range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = max(1, min(size, 30))
    coins = rng.sample(range(1, 60), k)
    amount = rng.randrange(0, 120)
    return [amount, coins]
